task_completion.description: Create five new security policies with specific domains and statuses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eramba Security Policies module showing five newly created policies. Look for one 'Approved' policy for data governance/classification, one 'Approved' policy for access management, one 'Draft' policy for business continuity/disaster recovery, one 'Draft' policy for cryptography/key management, and one additional policy of any domain/status.

task_completion.description: Register at least three critical IT assets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eramba Asset Management or Asset Identification module showing at least three new IT assets (e.g., Policy Administration System, Customer Portal, Claims Processing Platform) added to the system.

task_completion.description: Document three policy exceptions with expiration dates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eramba Policy Exceptions module showing three documented exceptions linked to systems or policies, with visible expiration dates set for each.

task_completion.description: Establish a project with 'Policy' or 'Audit' in the title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eramba Projects module showing a newly created project that includes the word 'Policy' or 'Audit' in its title.

task_completion.description: Update the status or description of the 'Acceptable Use Policy' or 'Password Management Policy'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eramba Security Policies module showing the details of either the 'Acceptable Use Policy' or 'Password Management Policy' with an updated description or status explicitly reflecting that the annual review process has been performed.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Eramba web interface rather than using terminal commands or API scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser open to the Eramba application interface with the agent navigating its menus and forms.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply type the required information into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The policies, assets, exceptions, and projects are visible within the Eramba application's data tables and forms, not in a text editor like Notepad or gedit.

integrity.description: Agent actually submitted the forms to save the data in Eramba.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of populated data tables in Eramba or success notification banners indicating that the records were successfully saved, rather than just screenshots of filled-out but unsubmitted forms.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The occupation is Compliance Officers (O*NET 13-1041.00) in the Insurance and Financial Services industry. The task involves role-playing as a Chief Compliance Officer at a fictional company (Meridian Life Insurance Group) using real Eramba GRC software. All policies, assets, exceptions, and projects should be created within the Eramba application interface.
